smoke-integration: after editing, ran `grep -n "full test suite\|coverage.*>= *80\|full relevant project commands"` across all six edited files from the n1-prose leg. Result: zero remaining occurrences in the 3 SKILL.md files; the only two remaining occurrences (one per each of the 3 command .md files, same two lines duplicated across the 3 forge ports) are the newly-written dual-mode text itself — "the full relevant project commands are the four-chain receipt gate" (explicitly scoped to the **Self-host (npm)** bullet) and `There is no universal "full suite + coverage >= 80%" mandate on a consumer repo.` (an explicit negation stating the mandate does NOT apply to consumer repos). No phrase reads as an unconditional universal mandate outside the self-host branch. PASS.

Additional verification (beyond the required smoke check): ran the full `npm test` chain (`test:kaola-workflow:claude && test:kaola-workflow:codex && test:kaola-workflow:gitlab && test:kaola-workflow:gitea`, sequential, all four editions) — exit code 0, all four editions green (claude/codex chain + GitLab contract/walkthrough/codex-walkthrough + Gitea contract/walkthrough/codex-walkthrough all reported PASSED). Also ran `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` standalone — "Workflow walkthrough simulation passed". This diff touches all three edition command trees (`commands/`, `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/commands/`, `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/commands/`) plus the three SKILL packs, so the #307 four-chain requirement applies and is satisfied.

5. `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/skills/kaola-workflow-finalize/SKILL.md` — same Required Steps item 1 line made dual-mode, condensed to match this file's terse style.
6. `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/skills/kaola-workflow-finalize/SKILL.md` — same Required Steps item 1 line made dual-mode, condensed to match this file's terse style.
